digest.component_or_area: operation-cert signing / attestation gate (rebar attest substrate)
propositions: Operation certificates currently use per-clone symmetric HMAC secrets and are only local self-attestations, not third-party-verifiable enforceable controls.
Migrate operation certificates to asymmetric Ed25519 signatures attributed to a signing environment under namespace rebar.opcert.v1, verifiable by anyone against the environment's public key.

A project can optionally require a certificate from a specific trusted environment, with keys pinned out-of-band in .rebar/trusted_environments.yaml on the Gerrit-gated code branch, never the auto-pushed tickets branch.

The merge gate must verify the required-environment signature over the merged log against the pinned key rather than the certificate's self-claimed key_id, extending the shipped verify-identity gate rather than a parallel walker.

Certificates bind {ticket id, material fingerprint, merged-log position} so they cannot be replayed onto another ticket or mutated state, using explicit-SHA era-scoped key validity and the shared key_not_valid_at_era verdict.

A trusted rebar-mcp remote must fetch authoritative ticket and code state itself and run the gate on that, so a developer cannot forge a passing verdict via doctored client inputs; the default local path stays offline and never wedges.
